2606.24381.title: On the Stability of Prompt Ranking in Large Language Model Evaluation
2606.24381.paper_summary: Prompt-based interaction has become a dominant paradigm for using large language models (LLMs), where multiple candidate prompts are evaluated and the top-ranked one is selected for downstream use. This workflow implicitly assumes that prompt rankings are stable under minor variations in evaluation conditions. In this paper, we systematically study prompt ranking stability under common sources of variability, including random seeds and limited evaluation subsets. Across three open-weight LLMs and two benchmark tasks, we find that while overall rank correlations are often moderate to high, the identity of the top-performing prompt frequently changes, leading to unreliable selection decisions. To address this issue, we propose a simple stability-aware selection strategy based on a lower confidence bound, which accounts for both performance and variance. Our results show that this approach improves robustness in unstable settings while remaining competitive in more stable regimes. These findings highlight the importance of accounting for evaluation uncertainty in prompt selection and LLM benchmarking.
2606.24381.paper_summary_zh: 基於提示的互動已成為使用大型語言模型（LLMs）的主導範式，在這個範式中，會評估多個候選提示，並選擇排名最高的提示用於下游應用。這個工作流程隱含地假設在評估條件的輕微變化下，提示的排名是穩定的。在本文中，我們系統地研究了在常見變異來源下的提示排名穩定性，包括隨機種子和有限的評估子集。在三個開放權重的LLM和兩個基準任務中，我們發現，儘管整體排名相關性通常為中等到高，但表現最佳的提示的身份經常變化，導致選擇決策不可靠。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一種基於較低置信界限的簡單穩定性意識選擇策略，該策略考慮了性能和方差。我們的結果顯示，這種方法在不穩定的環境中提高了穩健性，同時在更穩定的環境中保持競爭力。這些發現突顯了在提示選擇和LLM基準測試中考慮評估不確定性的重要性。
2606.24381.author: Shaoshuai Du et.al.
2606.24381.authors: Shaoshuai Du, Penghao Liang, Yixian Shen, Chuanqi Shi, Hang Zhang, Lun Wang
